summary: Russ Fein highlights that while quantum computing companies regularly announce technical milestones—such as higher qubit counts and improved error correction—there is still “no clear, independently verified evidence” of a quantum computer achieving a commercially useful result unattainable by classical computers. Fein presents his 2026 rankings of twelve leading companies, including IonQ, IBM, Google, and Quantinuum, prioritizing those showing credible progress toward scalable, manufacturable systems. IonQ stands out for surpassing 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity and notable acquisitions like SkyWater Technology, while IBM leads with its modular quantum processor roadmap and global network. The United States Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative aims to verify if any “utility-scale” quantum computer can economically outperform classical systems on useful problems by 2033, underscoring the importance of independent validation.
